Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.
  1 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

No `ease-in-out` literals. 259 8. **Pure presentational component.** No Redux/Zustand imports. Data flows in as props. The component does not read from any store. 260 9. **File must live at `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx`.** CHR-V3-02 will import `WorkspaceSwitcherChip` from a sibling file in the same directory. 261 10. **Export `TopBarV3Props` and `TopBarV3` as named exports.** `AppShell.tsx` will import the v3 surface by name under the design-system guard. 262 263 --- 264 265 ## Verify gate 266 267 ```bash 268 # Type-check 269 pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web tsc --noEmit 270 271 # Unit tests (new v3 block must pass; existing v2 block must not regress) 272 pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test -- --reporter=verbose packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx 273 274 # ESLint no-hex rule 275 pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web exec eslint packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx 276 277 # Full CI gate 278 pnpm verify 279 ``` 280 281 All four commands must exit 0 before the branch is push-ready. 282 283 --- 284 285 ## Commit and push 286 287 ```bash 288 git add packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx \ 289 packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx 290 git commit -m "feat(chrome): CHR-V3-01 top-bar v3 component — 8-slot, token-only colors, presence, Cmd+K/H bindings" 291 git push -u origin feat/v3-chr-v3-01-topbar 292 ``` 293 294 --- 295 296 ## Final report 297 298 Paste back to orchestrator when done: 299 300 ``` 301 WP: WP-014 / CHR-V3-01 302 Branch: feat/v3-chr-v3-01-topbar 303 Status: done 304 Tests: passed (v3 block: ; v2 block: unchanged) 305 Files changed: 306 packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx (new) 307 packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx (extended) 308 ESLint no-hex: pass 309 Notes: 310 ``` 311

Tests — extend `TopBar.test.tsx` Add a separate `describe('TopBar v3 — CHR-V3-01')` block in `packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx` (do not delete the existing v2 block): - Renders with `data-testid="topbar-v3"` at height token. - Presence: 4 avatars render; 8 presence → 4 avatars + "+4" overflow. - Empty presence → `data-testid="topbar-v3-presence"` is absent from DOM. - `onCommandPalette` fires on Cmd+K button click. - `onActivityStream` fires on activity button click. - `onActivityStream` fires on `Cmd+H` keydown on document. - Project name truncates at 180 px container and shows `title` attribute. - Workspace chip carries `data-disc="arch"` attribute when `activeWorkspaceId="arch"`. - No hex literal in inline styles (test by serialising `getComputedStyle` or checking rendered `style` attributes — assert none contain `#` outside a CSS variable reference). --- ## Implementation rules 1. **No hex literals in chrome.** The `bim-ai/no-hex-in-chrome` ESLint rule is live. Every color value must be a CSS variable: `var(--color-*)`, `var(--disc-*)`, `var(--brand-*)`. If you need ochre, write `var(--brand-accent)`. If a caller passes a presence color as a string, that string originates from the store — the component accepts it via prop but does not define hex values itself. Violations fail CI. 2. **No Tailwind classes for color.** Use `style={}` props for all color, height, and border declarations so the values trace back to tokens unambiguously. Tailwind layout utilities (`flex`, `items-center`, `gap-*`) are fine. 3. **`--radius-canvas: 0` on the bar root.** The top bar is a rail surface. The root `div` must have `borderRadius: 'var(--radius-canvas)'`. Interior elements (buttons, chips) may use `--radius-md` or `--radius-sm`. 4. **No tools in the top bar.** The top bar is identity + meta-actions only. Do not add authoring verb buttons. This is the A3 antidote. 5. **`--text-sm` for menus, `--text-base` for project name.** These are the two type sizes specified in the tracker. `--text-2xs` may appear in tooltip shortcut chips only. 6. **16 × 16 icon size** for all icon slots. Pass `size={16}` to `Icons.*`. 7. **`--ease-paper` for any transition.** If you add a `transition` property anywhere in the component, the easing must be `var(--ease-paper)` and duration `var(--motion-slow)` (240 ms). No `ease-in-out` literals. 8. **Pure presentational component.** No Redux/Zustand imports. Data flows in as props. The component does not read from any store. 9. **File must live at `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx`.** CHR-V3-02 will import `WorkspaceSwitcherChip` from a sibling file in the same directory. 10. **Export `TopBarV3Props` and `TopBarV3` as named exports.** `AppShell.tsx` will import the v3 surface by name under the design-system guard. --- ## Verify gate ```bash # Type-check pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web tsc --noEmit # Unit tests (new v3 block must pass; existing v2 block must not regress) pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test -- --reporter=verbose packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx # ESLint no-hex rule pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web exec eslint packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx # Full CI gate pnpm verify ``` All four commands must exit 0 before the branch is push-ready. --- ## Commit and push ```bash git add packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx \ packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx git commit -m "feat(chrome): CHR-V3-01 top-bar v3 component — 8-slot, token-only colors, presence, Cmd+K/H bindings" git push -u origin feat/v3-chr-v3-01-topbar ``` --- ## Final report Paste back to orchestrator when done: ``` WP: WP-014 / CHR-V3-01 Branch: feat/v3-chr-v3-01-topbar Status: done Tests: passed (v3 block: ; v2 block: unchanged) Files changed: packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx (new) packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx (extended) ESLint no-hex: pass Notes: ```

} from '@bim-ai/icons';

/**
 * BIM AI chrome icon set — the single import surface for icon usage outside
 * the canvas. BIM-specific icons come from `@bim-ai/icons`; generic chrome
 * icons (chevrons, close, search, etc.) come from `lucide-react`.
 *
 * Default visual rules (spec §10):
 *   - Default size 16 px in dense chrome; 18 px in tool palette; 20 px in
 *     TopBar. Pass `size` prop to override.
 *   - Default stroke width 1.5; selected state 2.0.
 *   - Color inherits `currentColor` so theme tokens drive it.
 *
 * Every icon-only button must pair its icon with `aria-label` (spec §22).
 */

export type LucideLikeIcon = ComponentType<
  SVGAttributes<SVGSVGElement> & {
    size?: number | string;
    strokeWidth?: number | string;
    absoluteStrokeWidth?: boolean;
  }
>;

/** @deprecated use StairIcon from @bim-ai/icons. Kept for test backwards compat. */
export { StairIcon as StairsIcon } from '@bim-ai/icons';

 * icons. Tools / shell / dialogs read from here so the assignments can be
 * audited as a unit. */
export const Icons: Record<string, LucideLikeIcon> = {
  // Selection & manipulation
  select: MousePointer2,

  // Views & documentation — BIM-native

  sectionView: SectionViewIcon,
  elevationView: ElevationViewIcon,
  orbitView: OrbitViewIcon,
  sheet: SheetIcon,
  schedule: ScheduleViewIcon,
  callout: CalloutIcon,
  viewpoint: ViewpointIcon,
  sectionBox: SectionBoxIcon,
  gridLine: GridLineIcon,
  level: LevelIcon,
  detailLine: DetailLineIcon,

  // Organization & data — BIM-native
  family: FamilyIcon,
  familyType: FamilyTypeIcon,
  group: GroupIcon,
  assembly: AssemblyIcon,
  linkedModel: LinkedModelIcon,
  material: MaterialIcon,
  wallLayer: WallLayerIcon,
  phase: PhaseIcon,

  // Coordination — BIM-native
  issue: IssueIcon,
  clash: ClashIcon,
  validationRule: ValidationRuleIcon,
  deviation: DeviationIcon,

  // Camera / view — lucide (generic)
  saveViewpoint: Camera,
  layerOn: Eye,
  layerOff: EyeOff,
  viewCubeReset: Home,

  // History — lucide (generic)
  undo: Undo2,

  // Theme — lucide (generic)
  themeLight: Sun,
  themeDark: Moon,

  // Navigation / global chrome — lucide (generic)
  commandPalette: Command,
  search: Search,
  settings: Settings2,
  hamburger: Menu,
  collaborators: Users,
  close: X,
  externalLink: ExternalLink,
  proportional: Link2,

  // Disclosure — lucide (generic)
  disclosureClosed: ChevronRight,

  // Status / signals — lucide (generic) + BIM-native

  advisorWarning: AlertTriangle,
  online: CircleDot,
  snap: Magnet,
  grid: Grid3x3,
};

/** Human-readable default labels for `aria-label` on icon-only buttons. */
export const IconLabels: Record<keyof typeof Icons, string> = {
  select: 'Select',

};

export type IconName = keyof typeof Icons;

/** Default icon sizes per spec §10. */
export const ICON_SIZE = {
  chrome: 16,
  toolPalette: 18,

} as const;

// Re-export all BIM icons so consumers can import them from @bim-ai/ui without
